title: What is Data Analytics?
concept: Data analytics = examining data to find patterns, answer questions, and guide decisions — turning raw data into insight. It's the foundation AI builds on.

exercises.prompt: Recap: "Big Data" means data that is…?

options.text: Too big, fast, or varied for a normal computer
Data printed in a large font

exercises.explain: Right — big data is about scale and complexity (the 3 V's).

cards.title: Raw data is useless on its own
cards.body: A spreadsheet of 10,000 sales means nothing until you ask questions of it. Asking those questions is analytics.

cards.title: So what is data analytics?
cards.body: Examining data to find patterns, answer questions, and guide decisions — turning raw numbers into insight.

cards.title: Real questions it answers
cards.body: "Which product sells best on weekends?" · "Why did users quit the app?" · "Which city is growing fastest?"

cards.title: Analytics vs AI
cards.body: Analytics is often humans + tools finding answers; AI/ML automates and scales that pattern-finding. They're cousins — analytics is the groundwork.

cards.title: From data to insight
cards.body: The whole point in one picture:

options.text: Examining data to find patterns and answer questions

exercises.explain: Analytics is about making sense of data — finding patterns and answering questions.
Analytics answers questions you can dig out of recorded data.

exercises.prompt: True or false: analytics turns raw data into useful insights.
exercises.explain: True — that conversion from raw data to insight is exactly the job.

exercises.sentence: Analytics that asks 'what will happen next?' is called ___ analytics.
exercises.explain: Predictive analytics forecasts what's likely to happen next.

exercises.prompt: An online store has tons of sales data but can't figure out why sales drop every Monday. What do they need?

options.text: Data analytics to examine the data and find the pattern

exercises.explain: They already have the data — they need analytics to turn it into the answer.
